message:
perf(codec): eliminate double-serialize in serialize_with_mode Auto path (ft-gbpoy)

Per the perf-hotspot audit at HEAD a9bdaa9e, serialize_with_mode in
CompressionMode::Auto path serialized the value TWICE for any PDU
above COMPRESS_THRESH (32 bytes):
  1. Once into `uncompressed` buffer for the size check.
  2. Again through `varbincode::Serializer::new(&mut zstd::Encoder)`
     for the actual compressed wire output.

The second serialize was wasteful: the already-encoded `uncompressed`
buffer can be fed straight into zstd's bulk-stream API.

Fix: replace the inner re-serialize with
`zstd::stream::encode_all(&uncompressed[..], DEFAULT_COMPRESSION_LEVEL)`.
Same wire output (varbincode bytes wrapped in zstd framing), half the
serialize work above the threshold.

Also added `Vec::with_capacity(64)` for the `uncompressed` buffer —
small win, avoids the first few realloc-grows for typical small PDUs.

Mechanics:
- frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs:467 — serialize_with_mode body simplified
  from a streaming-encoder pattern (zstd::Encoder + varbincode::Serializer
  → t.serialize → compress.finish) to a one-shot encode_all call. Net
  change: -5 LOC, +2 LOC.
- Compress path now: serialize once → encode_all the buffer. No
  double-serialize.

Build verification:
- cargo check -p codec: clean (24.47s, 2 unrelated warnings).
- cargo test -p codec serialize: 10 passed, 0 failed (including
  serialize_auto_mode_large_data, serialize_always_mode,
  serialize_never_mode roundtrip tests). Symmetry with the
  deserialize path verified — compressed payloads decode correctly.

Performance impact: for any PDU above 32 bytes (the COMPRESS_THRESH),
the serializer now runs once instead of twice. For small PDUs (<= 32
bytes) the Auto path was already single-serialize; behavior unchanged.

For large PDUs (mux client snapshots, render-changes, distributed
envelopes), expect ~50% reduction in serializer CPU on the encode
path. No microsecond benchmark gathered in this 60-min slot — the
fix is structural and the test suite verifies wire-compatibility.

Closes ft-gbpoy (P3 perf finding from the perf-hotspot audit).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
